steps.title: Identify the key signal about Lucia's understanding
steps.content: The critical detail is in the final sentence: “She knows from her teaching that this is not a good sign, the use of pencil intended to soften the indignity of the corrections.” Lucia understands that the penciled approach is a deliberate softening of criticism—gentle phrasing meant to cushion bad news.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C (‘She is familiar with the approach Rafael Méndez has taken and is therefore aware that it is meant to be a sensitive way of indicating the faults in her work.’) states her exact understanding: she is “aware that it is meant to be a sensitive way of indicating the faults.” The others misread the situation:
  • A treats sparse praise as merit-spotting
  • B invents gratitude and calls indirect feedback direct
  • D fabricates disagreement


That confirms C is correct.
